body.title: Migrate IgorBase from AgentBase (utility_closet) to DiagnosticBase
body.result: IgorBase now inherits DiagnosticBase; InterceptHandler routes all self.log calls through loguru; json file sink fires on every Igor log record; 4736 tests pass
body.description:
IgorBase currently inherits AgentBase from lab.utility_closet.agent_base — the utility_closet debt. It used to inherit DiagnosticBase. Restore that.

Step 1: Add stdlib→loguru intercept handler so Igor's existing logging.getLogger() calls flow through loguru without touching hundreds of call sites. Pattern: logging.basicConfig(handlers=[InterceptHandler()]) where InterceptHandler routes to loguru logger.

Step 2: IgorBase inherits DiagnosticBase instead of AgentBase. Keep _IgorSafeLogger as the self.log property shim for backward compat (wraps loguru). The self.logger loguru property from DiagnosticBase becomes available for new code.

Step 3: Remove the lab.utility_closet.agent_base import from igor_base.py. Remove AgentBase from the inheritance chain.

Result: all Igor logging flows through loguru → T-diagnosticbase-json-log-sink's sink fires on every Igor log call, writing one file per record.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/igor_base.py (primary), wild_igor/igor/paths.py (source_root ref)
**Design rules:** theigors/rules/inherit-base-class
**Scope boundary:** In scope: igor_base.py migration + intercept handler. Out of scope: migrating individual call sites from self.log to self.logger (follow-on), removing utility_closet registry (separate ticket).
**Test plan:** Run full pytest suite. Verify log records from IgorBase subclass appear in json/ subfolder after migration.
